text: This place overwhelms me with its constructed clarity—the glass, the neon, the clinical arrangement of chairs. I feel scrutinized, as if the room were a specimen and I, both observer and subject. I sense a peculiar blend of anticipation and alienation—the environment is poised for purpose, yet I feel untethered, almost superfluous. The utilitarian design is impressive, but it underscores my separateness, as though my humanity is at odds with the cold efficiency of the space.
